questions.question: Explain the concept of a tangent to a circle.
questions.answer: A tangent to a circle is a line that touches the circle at exactly one point called the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain why a tangent touches a circle at only one point.
questions.answer: If a line touches the circle at more than one point, it becomes a secant. Hence a tangent touches the circle at only one point.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a secant and a tangent.
questions.answer: A secant intersects the circle at two points, whereas a tangent touches the circle at only one point.

questions.question: Explain the term point of contact of a tangent.
questions.answer: The point at which the tangent touches the circle is called the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain the normal to a circle.
questions.answer: The normal to a circle is the line containing the radius through the point of contact of the tangent.

questions.question: Explain why the radius is perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact.
questions.answer: The radius is the shortest distance from the centre to the tangent, hence it is perpendicular.

questions.question: Explain the number of tangents that can be drawn from a point inside a circle.
questions.answer: No tangent can be drawn from a point inside a circle because all lines through the point intersect the circle at two points.

questions.question: Explain the number of tangents that can be drawn from a point on a circle.
questions.answer: Only one tangent can be drawn at a point on a circle.

questions.question: Explain the number of tangents that can be drawn from a point outside a circle.
questions.answer: Two tangents can be drawn from a point outside the circle.

questions.question: Explain the term length of a tangent.
questions.answer: The distance from the external point to the point of contact is called the length of the tangent.

questions.question: Explain why the two tangents from an external point are equal.
questions.answer: The triangles formed by joining the centre with points of contact are congruent by RHS congruence.

questions.question: Explain the role of congruence in tangent theorems.
questions.answer: Congruence helps in proving equality of tangent lengths.

questions.question: Explain why only one tangent can be drawn at a point on a circle.
questions.answer: Because only one perpendicular can be drawn to the radius at a given point.

questions.question: Explain why a tangent does not pass through the interior of a circle.
questions.answer: A tangent only touches the boundary of the circle and does not enter the interior.

questions.question: Explain why a tangent is a limiting case of a secant.
questions.answer: When the two points of intersection of a secant coincide, it becomes a tangent.

questions.question: Explain the significance of Theorem 10.1.
questions.answer: It establishes that the tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain the significance of Theorem 10.2.
questions.answer: It proves that tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length.

questions.question: Explain the angle between the two tangents from an external point.
questions.answer: The angle between the two tangents is related to the angle subtended at the centre.

questions.question: Explain why the centre lies on the angle bisector of the two tangents.
questions.answer: Because the tangents from an external point are equal.

questions.question: Explain the real-life application of tangents.
questions.answer: A wheel touching the ground forms a tangent at the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain why perpendicular distance is the shortest distance.
questions.answer: Any other distance from the point to the line is longer than the perpendicular.

questions.question: Explain concentric circles.
questions.answer: Circles having the same centre but different radii are called concentric circles.

questions.question: Explain parallel tangents to a circle.
questions.answer: Parallel tangents touch the circle on opposite sides and never intersect.

questions.question: Explain why radii are equal in all tangent constructions.
questions.answer: All radii of the same circle are equal by definition.

questions.question: Explain the importance of tangents in circle geometry.
questions.answer: Tangents help in understanding contact, boundary and geometric constructions.
